name: how-to-create-claude-code-skill
description: A guide to creating Claude Code Skills.
capabilities: Explains required SKILL.md frontmatter fields and sections
Lists optional directories and files for complex skills
Documents personal and project skill storage paths
Provides file reference and script call examples
author: ClaudeCode-NPC
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only markdown guidance. No executable code, scripts, network activity, or file system operations are present. All 26 static findings are false positives: the scanner misidentified YAML metadata fields as cryptographic terms, documentation URLs as hardcoded endpoints, and example command syntax as actual shell execution.
valueStatement: You need clear guidance to build Claude Code skills that trigger correctly. This guide explains required files, naming conventions, and folder organization patterns so you can write compliant skills that work as expected.
userTitle: Create Claude Code skills with proper structure
limitations: No automation or scripts are included
No validation or packaging instructions are provided
No tool integrations or APIs are described
Examples are descriptive only, not runnable

useCases.title: Start a new skill
useCases.description: Use the guide to draft a compliant SKILL.md and pick a safe folder layout.

useCases.title: Standardize skills
useCases.description: Apply naming and organization guidance to keep team skills consistent.

useCases.title: Refine an existing skill
useCases.description: Check structure, references, and limits against documented requirements.

promptTemplates.title: List required files
promptTemplates.prompt: List the required files and frontmatter fields for a Claude Code skill.

promptTemplates.title: Choose a structure
promptTemplates.prompt: Propose a folder structure for a skill with references and helper scripts.

promptTemplates.title: Naming guidance
promptTemplates.prompt: Suggest a skill name that follows the naming rules and explain why.

promptTemplates.title: Progressive disclosure
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain how to split content between SKILL.md and reference files.

bestPractices: Keep SKILL.md under 500 lines for performance
Link to reference files instead of duplicating details
antiPatterns: Adding extra documentation files like README or CHANGELOG
Using vague skill names such as Helper or Tools
Deeply nested references beyond one level

faq.question: Is this compatible with Claude and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance is tool-agnostic and mentions Claude Code layouts.

faq.question: What limits should I follow?
faq.answer: Keep SKILL.md under 500 lines and respect name and description length limits.

faq.question: Does it integrate with other tools?
faq.answer: No. It is a documentation-only guide without tool integrations.

faq.question: Does it handle sensitive data safely?
faq.answer: It does not access data, files, or networks, so it does not process sensitive data.

faq.question: What if my skill does not trigger?
faq.answer: Review the frontmatter description and ensure it clearly states when to use the skill.

faq.question: How does it compare to a full template generator?
faq.answer: It provides guidance only and does not generate or validate files.
